channel.title: maGaming RSS Feed - insider-gaming.com
channel.description: A cleaned-up version of the original gaming feed for insider-gaming.com

item.title: Embark Addresses ‘False Bans’ in ARC Raiders Tied to ‘Accessibility Devices’
item.content:encoded:
Embark Studios has posted a message on social media and Discord announcing its intention to look into recent bans handed out to players of ARC Raiders using an accessibility peripheral. In the recent post, the team said they’re ‘reviewing all appeals’, which, presumably, means they’re trudging through the gripes with a human eye.

In the interim, the team directed players to the Embark support platform, where they can request an appeal of a recent ban decision. This isn’t the first time players have complained about facing ‘false bans’ in ARC Raiders.

Have You Been Banned in ARC Raiders?

For weeks, if not longer, players have talked about being banned ‘for no reason’ in ARC Raiders. It was a theme that surfaced more prominently as the new year kicked off, with Embark Studios returning from a festive break and promptly wading into a sea of cheaters and malicious operators.

On social media, countless players, including some of the biggest streamers in the business, reported being banned from ARC Raiders.

In a new post, Embark has once again said it’s looking at ARC Raiders bans that might be false and not indicative of punishing a real cheater:

It’s come to our attention that certain players using accessibility peripherals are getting banned in the game. We are working hard on resolving this issue as soon as possible.

Our support team is reviewing all appeals, please continue to reach out to us if this has happened to you.

Thank you for bringing this to our attention.

The team then shared the link to the Embark support page that details how to get your account unbanned, if you’re lucky, by submitting an official appeal. It reads:

If your account has received a restriction, this guide explains how the appeal process works, what you can expect during a review, and where to find more information about our enforcement policies.

At the end of the appeals process, your ban will either be upheld, adjusted, or lifted.

On Reddit, some users praised Embark Studios for taking decisive action against bans that are removing players who need more accessibility measures from the game. Many third-party inputs and software components conflict with anti-cheat systems, and this has long been an issue.

It doesn’t help that most of these peripherals have been reworked by malicious operators to seek an advantage over ‘legitimate’ players.

That’s where the opposite side of the fence resided, with many suggesting that if Embark Studios becomes more lenient on accessibility devices, they open the door to more cheaters.

Have you received a ‘false ban’ in ARC Raiders? Let us know on the Insider Gaming Discord server if you’ve raised an appeal.

For more Insider Gaming coverage, check out the news about the recent ARC Raiders patch

Senior Editor and Esports Lead

Grant has been gaming for 30+ years and in the industry for 10+. You'll probably find him playing a post-apocalyptic game or an extraction shooter somewhere.

More from Grant Taylor-Hill

MOBILE APP

Level Up Your Gaming News

Real-time news, exclusive podcasts, push alerts, and a better reading experience.

https://insider-gaming.com/arc-raiders-embark-addresses-false-bans-accessibility/
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: ARC Raiders Patch Nerfs Il Toro and Cuts Down Energy Clip Price
item.content:encoded:
Embark Studios has just deployed Patch 1.20.0 in ARC Raiders, nerfing one of the most overpowered weapons in the game and adding a few more customization elements. The Il Toro has been the bane of some players’ lives for the longest time, mostly because it can slam you from a ludicrous distance for a homemade shotgun, but that’s (hopefully) a thing of the past.

With this update, players can also access the Rawhide Outfit and two new haircuts, building on the recent addition of beards and moustaches to ARC Raiders.

Cutting Down the ARC Economy

Embark Studios has pushed out 1.20.0, a minor ARC Raiders patch that adds two new haircuts, a sweet outfit, and a few balancing-based adjustments.

The heart of the update centers on managing the Il Toro shotgun, which has been viciously overpowered in recent weeks.

Per the update notes:

This is an adjustment many of you requested. We have tuned the Il Toro to address its DPS and effectiveness at range, especially when combined with a choke. We’re also making some changes so it performs more like a weapon of its rarity (less versatile at lower upgrade levels and more dependent on upgrades and mods).

Most notably, fire-rate and reload time have been reduced, which will make it more important to fully utilize positioning, cover, and timing to defeat your opponents. We will of course continue to keep a close eye on this to see how it plays out in practice, and make additional adjustments when needed. 

Everything from pellet damage to dispersion has been adjusted in the 1.20.0 patch, reducing the effectiveness of this point-blank nightmare, which had become less point-blank in the last few weeks. For instance, the reload time for the Il Toro has been increased from 4.3 to 5.7 seconds, which is a considerable jump.

Also, the selling price of Energy Clips has been adjusted after Embark Studios noticed how lucrative crafting them had become. They’re now selling for just 200 coins, down from 1000 coins. That’s a massive decrease, and it’s because of the ‘unintentional’ market value they generated following a recent update.

Embark also fixed a pesky audio bug tied to destroyed Comets and Fireballs, adjusted a wall-clipping bug in the new Controlled Access Zone on Dam, and tweaked more collision issues across Spaceport.

The team also revealed that a new Wasp Hunter Set is coming soon:

That’s about it for 1.20.0, but the game’s most major update yet sits on the near horizon. In April, we’ll be getting our first new ARC Raiders map since Stella Montis was released, and a new large-scale ARC enemy will enter the game.

Let us know on the Insider Gaming Discord server if you’re happy with this ARC Raiders update.

For more Insider Gaming coverage, check out the news that more Xbox Game Pass titles have been unveiled

Senior Editor and Esports Lead

Grant has been gaming for 30+ years and in the industry for 10+. You'll probably find him playing a post-apocalyptic game or an extraction shooter somewhere.

More from Grant Taylor-Hill

item.title: “I think it’s frustrating for anyone”: TeSeS on Fixing Falcons Errors
item.content:encoded:
Insider Gaming spoke to Falcons’ rifler René ‘TeSeS’ Madsen before the start of their BLAST Open Rotterdam 2026 campaign, which sees the roster aim to recover from an error-ridden start to the team’s Counter-Strike year.

A quarter-final exit at PGL Cluj-Napoca to Parivision once again highlighted the team’s difficulties in closing advantageous scenarios, but after skipping ESL Pro League Season 23, Falcons are hoping to put to bed their characteristic mistakes.

Obviously, you guys skipped Pro League. What were you working on during that time when everyone else was at the event or playing online?

Well, we tried to pinpoint the crucial things that we can improve on, everything from discipline to communication, and also helping each other when we’re dead, for example.

I think one of the most important points was probably the discipline part and considerations in crucial rounds when we want to play more as a team and have a better connection with each other.

Going back to Cluj, what were the specific things you were looking at after that series with Parivision?

We definitely lost some crucial rounds and some advantages that we never should have. So, of course, we had to figure out how to improve that, first of all, and that’s what we tried to do.

Also, we weren’t able to help each other in the server when we were playing, and not connecting with each other. That’s probably a few main things.

Individually, you’ve had a tough couple of events. What are you working on? Do you think that’s more of a team issue, that you’re struggling to have impact, or do you think it’s more about how you work together?

I don’t think the amount of hours is what I need to work on, but I don’t think it’s the team either. I think it’s mostly up to myself when we’re playing and the priorities I need to have when we’re playing.

When we started out with this team, I was all in for the team, and there was nothing I wouldn’t do. I was able to sacrifice everything. We found out pretty quickly that this is not how I can work or how it works for this team.

Now I’m working on prioritising myself a little bit more. Not sacrificing all the time, but also putting myself into good situations and being able to close out rounds pretty well.

I haven’t shown this too much in tournaments yet, but I know I have it. I know I can do it, and that is what I want to show.

That’s interesting. What does that mean? In terms of specifics on a map, are you going to take over some roles or take more aggressive peaks, or do you want to take more responsibility in the server?

There’s not going to be any role changes, but there’s definitely going to be more options for myself to be able to set me up or be able to play on more parameters than what we have done in the past.

As you said, you’ve been a very selfless player for Falcons, and had the most success in a team that was very system-based rather than individual-based. Do you think it’s more frustrating for you to see those kinds of errors when players are kind of overpeeking, overheating, because you understand how to play good system-based Counter-Strike?

Yeah, I think it’s frustrating for anyone, not just me. I’ve also done it myself. I’m not perfect either. So I think every time you watch it back, you’re like, why would I ever do that? And I think everyone in the team is like that.

So I don’t think putting your mind to frustration is the solution. That’s at least what I want to do in my CS career: try to help each other fix these things. I know everyone has it in them. We just need to fix it.

In terms of the team dynamics, we’ve seen this issue emerge where it’s been difficult to rein in the players. Is there something that has been instilled in the last couple of weeks within the players where kyxsan is going to be able to have the control to say, ‘No, don’t go for that,’ or to have more say in the round?

I think it’s more an overall thing in the mid-rounds and the situations where the people that have an overview when they’re dead, for example, anyone can help, like peek on each other, go back, play time, play 1-1, and stuff like this. This is what we have tried to work on since Cluj.

Before this Cluj event, and also at Cluj, we haven’t been doing it that much, and we haven’t been that comfortable with it, and that’s maybe why we throw away some of these clutch situations, because we lose overview in the round, or we’re not taking the best decision available.

This is one solution we’re going with. At least in practise, it’s been going a lot better, but we also need to be able to do it in the mid-game.

In terms of the map pool, are there any reflections coming out of Cluj?

Yeah, our map pool right now is a little bit all over the place, also for ourselves. We can have really good games on certain maps, and the next day, it can be the complete opposite.

But I also think that’s how it is nowadays in this competitive environment. Some teams can make a really good game plan against you on your best map, and sometimes you just run into a wall, but we definitely need to learn our map pool.

From the outside, you can see it’s a bit weird, but we’re trying to do it step by step like any other team.

Obviously, Pro League took place. I’m sure you guys were working hard, not sitting on your sofa watching it, but did you get to see any of it, or were there any reflections from the event that you took away?

No, I didn’t watch too much of it. Obviously, I saw NAVI won, Astralis got third place. I’m surprised that Spirit didn’t make it further. I think that’s probably the main thing.

Going into NRG in your first match, how are you feeling about those guys?

It’s a decent match-up, obviously not the strongest opponents that are here, but no matter how we look at it nowadays, we cannot underestimate anyone.

So it’s an exciting game, and we’ll just see how it goes. We will do some preparations tonight and be ready for the game tomorrow morning.

Do you think Falcons can overcome their clutch problems? You can discuss that on Insider Gaming’s Discord!

For more Counter-Strike news, see how NAVI secured the ESL Pro League Season 23 trophy.

Darragh is an Esports Journalist for Insider Gaming specialising in Counter-Strike. He loves to explore how esports teams work, or why they very often do not.

More from Darragh Harbinson

MOBILE APP

Level Up Your Gaming News

Real-time news, exclusive podcasts, push alerts, and a better reading experience.

Available on iOS & Android

item.title: Xbox Game Pass March Wave 2 Includes Disco Elysium & Resident Evil 7
item.content:encoded:
The second wave of titles coming to Xbox Game Pass in March has been revealed, including some incredibly popular titles such as Disco Elysium and Resident Evil 7: Biohazard.

This follows the very successful first wave for March, which added Cyberpunk 2077 to the service after numerous social media teases.

Disco Elysium & Resident Evil 7 Coming To Game Pass

Announced on the Xbox Wire, Disco Elysium and Resident Evil 7: Biohazard headline the second wave of Game Pass additions for March, arriving alongside 10 other titles.

Xbox continues to add to its classic line-up of RPGs with Final Fantasy 4 coming to the service, whilst South of Midnight and Clair Obscur: Expedition 33 arrive for Premium users in the near future.

Here are all of the games coming this month, alongside the dates they’ll become available and the required subscription tier.

  • March 17: DreamWorks Gabby’s Dollhouse: Ready to Party (Cloud, Xbox Series X|S, and PC)—Game Pass Ultimate, Game Pass Premium, PC Game Pass
  • March 18: South of Midnight (Cloud, Console, Handheld, and PC)—Game Pass Premium, Game Pass Ultimate, PC Game Pass
  • March 18: The Alters (Cloud, Xbox Series X|S, and PC)—Game Pass Premium, Game Pass Ultimate, PC Game Pass
  • March 19: Disco Elysium (Cloud, Xbox Series X|S, and PC)—Game Pass Ultimate, Game Pass Premium, PC Game Pass
  • March 24: Like a Dragon: Infinite Wealth (Cloud, Console, Handheld, and PC)—Game Pass Ultimate, Game Pass Premium, PC Game Pass
  • March 25: Absolum (Cloud, Xbox Series X|S, and PC)—Game Pass Ultimate, Game Pass Premium, PC Game Pass
  • March 26: Nova Roma (Game Preview) (PC)—Game Pass Ultimate, PC Game Pass
  • March 30: The Long Dark (Cloud, Console, and PC)—Game Pass Ultimate, Game Pass Premium, PC Game Pass
  • March 31: Resident Evil 7: Biohazard (Cloud, Console, and PC)—Game Pass Ultimate, Game Pass Premium, PC Game Pass
  • April 2: Barbie Horse Trails (Cloud, Console, Handheld, and PC)—Game Pass Ultimate, Game Pass Premium, PC Game Pass
  • April 2: Clair Obscur Expedition 33 (Cloud, Xbox Series X|S, Handheld, and PC)—Game Pass Premium, Game Pass Ultimate, PC Game Pass
  • April 7: Final Fantasy 4 (Cloud, Xbox Series X|S, and PC)—Game Pass Ultimate, Game Pass Premium, PC Game Pass

Xbox Game Pass Removals March 31

As usual, some games will be leaving the service to make way for the new titles on the horizon. Thankfully, it’s a small roster this time around, with two games making way, including:

  • Peppa Pig World Adventures (Cloud, Console, PC)
  • Mad Streets (Cloud, Console, PC)

Will you be playing any of the new titles joining Xbox Game Pass? Share your thoughts in the official Insider Gaming Discord Server.

For more news, check out the latest Pokémon Pokopia update, as well as the brand new Crimson Desert PS5 footage.

MOBILE APP

Level Up Your Gaming News

Real-time news, exclusive podcasts, push alerts, and a better reading experience.

Available on iOS & Android

item.title: New Pokémon Pokopia Update Fixes Many Broken Quests—Full Patch Notes
item.content:encoded:
Pokémon Pokopia might be the best Pokémon game in years, maybe ever, but it’s not without its flaws. One of the most notable downsides is certain Requests not behaving as intended, leaving players unable to progress—a new Pokémon Pokopia looks to fix this.

Having some Request trouble in Pokémon Pokopia? Ditto. Certain Requests have been as troublesome as high-level Pokémon when you don’t have enough Gym badges—they won’t listen.

Pokémon Pokopia Fixes Several Key Issues

Nintendo is looking to fix various Pokémon Pokopia Requests, such as Withered Wasteland, Bleak Beach, and Rocky Ridges, to overcome unlock conditions and progression issues.

If any of these quests have been giving you grief, help is on the way. Below are the confirmed patch notes and what the original problem was.

All Pokémon Pokopia March 17 Patch Notes

Confirmed Issues

  • During the Withered Wasteland request “Help make a home!”, Squirtle moves to the top of a tree and cannot be spoken to, causing it to become impossible to proceed with the request.
  • During the Bleak Beach request “Find the Pokémon Center!”, if you break the cracked block in the bridge before Professor Tangrowth crosses it, it becomes difficult to proceed with the request.
  • During the Bleak Beach request “Find the Pokémon Center!”, if you perform actions in a certain order, Professor Tangrowth’s bridge repair event will not trigger, causing it to become impossible to proceed with the request.
  • If you perform actions in a certain order at Rocky Ridges, the event in which you meet Rotom will not trigger.
  • If the Rocky Ridges request “Clear the path!” is triggered under certain conditions, it becomes difficult to proceed with the request.
  • Spinarak’s type is listed incorrectly in the Pokédex.

Plans for Improvement

We plan to implement improvements regarding the following:

  • During the Withered Wasteland request “Rock Smash your way to treasure!”, if you place another block at the same location as the cracked block close to Hitmonchan, it will make it hard to see how you should proceed.
  • During the Bleak Beach request “To Snorlax”, if you place another block at the same location as the cracked block close to Snorlax, it will make it hard to see how you should proceed.

Plans for Future Support

We plan to distribute the update data with fixes for the above issues in the near future.

If you have encountered one of these issues in your copy of the game, it will be resolved if you download the update data.

We will continue to investigate other issues.

Credit to Nintendo for the patch notes.

Will any of these changes come as a welcome relief for your save file? Are any other Requests being overlooked, or issues in general? Comment your thoughts via the Insider Gaming Discord.

Make sure you read about what happened to the Humans in Pokémon Pokopia, if you haven’t already. We also have more handy guides, including Pokémon Pokopia Codes.

MOBILE APP

Level Up Your Gaming News

Real-time news, exclusive podcasts, push alerts, and a better reading experience.

item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: PlayStation Portal Update Adds New ‘High Quality Mode’ & More In March Update
item.content:encoded:
March 2026 brings a brand-new PlayStation Portal update. The most significant improvement is the new 1080p High Quality mode, which will make your gameplay smoother than butter and more glorious than a beautifully manicured garden.

The PlayStation Portal has been a great success for Sony. The ability to play PS5 games on the go is a no-brainer, and Sony continues to support the device. A March 2026 update has added a huge new feature and made other vital tweaks.

1080p High Quality Mode & Several Enhancements In PlayStation Portal Update

A March 17 PlayStation Blog discussed the increasing presence of the PlayStation Portal and how more people than ever are engaging with it: “Cloud Streaming monthly users grew by 162% year-over-year in January, and over 50% of PS Portal users are now PlayStation Plus Premium subscribers.”

But you didn’t come here for that; you came for news on the 1080p High-Quality Mode.

Once the update is installed, you can choose an upgraded 1080p High Quality mode during Remote Play and Cloud Streaming, which ‘prioritizes higher video quality and uses more data.’

This should be a nice improvement over the 1080p Standard mode. Also, Sony has announced improvements to the overall streaming experience: Product pages, game invites, trophy information, and search functions have all been enhanced for greater accessibility and navigation.

Finally, the update now gives new players a ‘smoother onboarding experience’ to get you suited and booted in a flash.

The update goes live on March 18, and you can start enjoying all the new features right away!

Are you enjoying the PlayStation Portal? What else does Sony need to address in future updates? Share your ideas via the Insider Gaming Discord. You never know who’s listening!

Check out the first Crimson Desert PlayStation 5 console gameplay footage and Starfield and ‘Future Bethesda Titles’ using AI-Driven DLSS 5 Technology for more hot gaming news.

MOBILE APP

Level Up Your Gaming News

Real-time news, exclusive podcasts, push alerts, and a better reading experience.

Available on iOS & Android

item.title: First Crimson Desert PlayStation 5 Console Gameplay Footage Officially Revealed
item.content:encoded:
Crimson Desert looks like one of 2026’s most ambitious games, in a year with GTA 6 after all! Pearl Abyss has marketed Crimson Desert well, but the noticeable lack of PlayStation and Xbox console footage has been a huge worry. The wait is finally over.

With the Crimson Desert review embargo looming, everyone has been wondering how the game performs. Does it run great on PC but not on console? The gaming community has been burned by similar situations before, but if you’re wondering how Crimson Desert handles on console, you can stop imagining it and start watching it.

Crimson Desert PS5 Gameplay Finally Shown Off

The official PlayStation Japan YouTube channel has unveiled over 20 minutes of PlayStation 5 console gameplay footage ahead of the game’s much-hyped launch.

Pearl Abyss has previously said that the company is ‘not hiding anything.’

Now, it definitely isn’t. The main X page let players know: “#CrimsonDesert gameplay on the PlayStation 5 base console has premiered on PlayStation Japan’s PLAY! PLAY! PLAY! Along with a new boss..!”

You can check it out for yourself below.

One misconception to clean up is the comments being turned off for the video. This is pretty much a default action on all PlayStation Japan YouTube videos, and not something done specifically for Crimson Desert.

Has everyone been worrying about nothing? How does Crimson Desert look on PS5? Share your thoughts via the Insider Gaming Discord.

Also, check out if Crimson Desert runs on the Steam Deck and how to earn Crimson Desert Twitch Drops.

MOBILE APP

Level Up Your Gaming News

Real-time news, exclusive podcasts, push alerts, and a better reading experience.

Available on iOS & Android



https://insider-gaming.com/first-crimson-desert-playstation-5-console-gameplay-footage-officially-revealed/
